title: IIOS Action Request
description: A request submitted to the Governance API for classification and policy evaluation. Not an execution instruction: submitting this schema never causes an action to occur.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this request. Distinct from idempotency_key: two requests with the same idempotency_key are the same logical request replayed.

idempotency_key.description: Caller-supplied key. Governance MUST return the original decision for a repeated key within the idempotency window instead of re-evaluating or duplicating a capability.

correlation_id.description: Propagated unchanged across policy decision, approval, capability token and every resulting audit event.

classifier_hint.description: Non-authoritative suggestion only. The Policy Engine MUST NOT treat this as the final class; see docs/21_GOVERNANCE_CORE_SPEC.md Action Classifier.

produced_by.description: Model or component that produced the hint, e.g. 'model:sonnet-class' or 'rule:static-classifier'.

items.description: One or more atomic action items. Request-level class is the maximum (strictest) class across items unless a policy explicitly declares the request decomposable.

decomposable.description: If true, Governance MAY approve a subset of items independently. Default false: all-or-nothing.

on_behalf_of.description: Set when a runtime acts under a delegated scope. Never implies expanded authority beyond the delegation record.

actionClass.description: A=automatic read-only, B=controlled reversible, C=explicit human approval, D=prohibited in current phase. Constitution Article IV.

verb.description: Machine-readable verb, e.g. 'read', 'write', 'execute', 'deploy', 'merge', 'rotate', 'send', 'delete'. For secret-related requests, always one of the two distinct verbs 'secret_value.read' (disclosure; no class permits it) or 'secret_reference.use' (Class C, scoped use without disclosure) — never a generic 'read'/'access' on a secret resource_type. See docs/21_GOVERNANCE_CORE_SPEC.md 'Secret handling: value vs. reference'.

resource_ref.description: Concrete reference to the resource, e.g. repo/branch path, secret name, connector id. Never the secret value itself.

reason.description: Required for Class C and D items; recommended for all.

risk_signals.description: Attributes that may trigger reclassification, e.g. 'contains_secret_reference', 'touches_production', 'financial_write', 'irreversible'.
